author: w3Lb
content: WHY: ripgrep-level reach means 'cargo install ripsed' and a brew formula, not 'clone and build'. SCOPE — checklist, each item independently shippable: (1) crates.io: verify the ripsed name is available (squatting check FIRST — if taken, decide fallback name before anything else); publish order ripsed-core -> ripsed-fs -> ripsed-json -> ripsed -> ripsed-cli (path+version deps already set up correctly in workspace Cargo.toml); add publish dry-run to dev-release skill flow; keywords/categories/description metadata on every crate; README badge. (2) Homebrew: tap repo (dollspace-gay/homebrew-ripsed) with formula pointing at release tarballs + sha256 (release.yml already emits checksums). (3) scoop manifest + AUR PKGBUILD (can be community-maintained later; document in README). (4) cargo-deb in release workflow for a .deb artifact. HUMAN STEPS: crates.io token, tap repo creation, AUR account — flag these in the issue when worked. DEPENDS loosely on #101 (artifacts should include completions before wide distribution).
